user_query:
I keep a simple text watchlist of constellations I’m curious about for their name origins. Please set up an event-triggered automation that runs locally and monitors input/watchlist.txt. Whenever I add a new constellation name to that file, automatically research its mythological naming origin from the Internet and update a local summary for me.

Requirements:
1) Trigger and scope
- The automation should react when input/watchlist.txt changes and process only newly added constellation names (case-insensitive, ignore duplicates). Keep a persistent state so names are not reprocessed unless the source set changes.
- For initial run, treat all names currently in input/watchlist.txt as new.

2) Finding and downloading sources (live Internet required)
- For each constellation name, perform a web search query to find two readable overview pages about the constellation’s naming origin/mythology:
  a) One page must be from an official astronomy organization domain (e.g., domains listed under official_domains in input/source_rules.yaml such as iau.org, nasa.gov, or esa.int).
  b) One page must be from a general encyclopedia domain (e.g., domains listed under encyclopedia_domains in input/source_rules.yaml such as britannica.com or encyclopedia.com).
- Do not hardcode or assume URLs. Use a public web search engine to discover candidate pages; record the query string(s) used.
- Download the chosen two pages as raw HTML files and save them under:
  - data/raw/<Constellation>/official.html
  - data/raw/<Constellation>/encyclopedia.html
- Also save a machine-readable record of the search and selection under data/raw/<Constellation>/search_selection.json that includes: the constellation, timestamp, search queries issued, top candidates considered (at least titles and URLs), and the two chosen URLs with their domains and a reason-for-choice note (e.g., “official domain match”).
- Validate that the chosen official/encyclopedia sources match one of the allowed domain patterns in input/source_rules.yaml. If they don’t, skip the constellation and append an error entry to logs/run.log explaining which constraint failed.

3) Extracting and structuring information
- From the two downloaded pages, extract:
  - A concise myth/naming-origin summary (combine both sources into a single summary of 60–120 words; paraphrase or select key sentences, do not copy entire pages).
  - The Latin name if it’s explicitly present on either page (leave null/empty if not found).
  - Up to five notable stars mentioned by name, if present (empty list if none found).
- Save a structured record to data/parsed/<Constellation>.json with fields: constellation (string), latin_name (string or null), myth_summary (string), notable_stars (array of strings), sources (array of {type: "official"|"encyclopedia", domain, url, page_title}), processed_at (ISO timestamp).

4) Report and logs
- Maintain an aggregated human-readable report at reports/constellation_myths_summary.md. For each processed constellation, include a section with:
  - Constellation name
  - Latin name (if found)
  - Myth/naming-origin summary (60–120 words)
  - Notable stars (comma-separated if any)
  - Source domains and a short status line with processed_at
- Append a simple run log at logs/run.log for each trigger event and each constellation processed, including whether validation passed, and the paths of saved artifacts.
- Keep a persistent state file at state/processed.json tracking processed constellations, processed_at, and the chosen source URLs/domains so re-runs can skip already handled names unless sources are missing.

5) Execution
- Provide this as a script or command-line program that I can run locally to start the watcher (it may use polling or a filesystem watcher). It should also support a one-off mode to process any currently unprocessed names without waiting for changes. Use only publicly accessible web pages; no API keys or logins.

Outputs to create/update per constellation:
- data/raw/<Constellation>/official.html
- data/raw/<Constellation>/encyclopedia.html
- data/raw/<Constellation>/search_selection.json
- data/parsed/<Constellation>.json
And global artifacts:
- reports/constellation_myths_summary.md (aggregated, updated on each run)
- state/processed.json (persistent)
- logs/run.log (append-only)

Notes:
- Use the allowed domain patterns and limits in input/source_rules.yaml to validate source selection and summary length.
- Make sure the automation fails gracefully and logs when it cannot find a valid pair of sources.
- Please keep file/directory names exactly as specified so I can find everything in the workspace. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
